etymology.description: semantic 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 中(中) (zhōng) -> rush of water
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") for its semantic element, conveying the idea of fluid movement, with 中(中) (zhōng) to provide the phonetic element, approximating the sound 'chōng'. Water naturally rushes or flows, leading to the idea of a rapid movement or 'rush'.
etymology.simplification: 沖(沖) was simplified to 冲 by retaining 中(中) (zhōng, "middle") as it is, but reducing 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") to 冲's simpler top-stroke form.

examples.chinese: 他匆匆冲出门(他匆匆沖出門)。
examples.english: He rushed out of the door in a hurry.

examples.chinese: 清晨的海浪冲上沙滩(清晨的海浪沖上沙灘)。
examples.pinyin: Qīngchén de hǎilàng chōng shàng shātān.
examples.english: The morning waves rushed onto the beach.

examples.chinese: 孩子们冲向操场(孩子們沖向操場)，欢呼雀跃(歡呼雀躍)。
examples.pinyin: Háizimen chōng xiàng cāochǎng, huānhū què yuè.
examples.english: The children rushed to the playground, cheering joyfully.
